This is likely because UALink switches aren't finalized and are pending validation and qualification by AMD’s AI customers.The biggest advantage of using UALink over Ethernet is that AMD can build Helios using an existing, widely supported ecosystem of validated and qualified components. Ethernet switching ASICs, cables, and other ingredients are already used by hyperscalers and cloud providers worldwide, which accelerates deployment. You may like AMD's Enterprise CPU and GPU roadmap: Venice, Verano, Zen 6, Helios, and CDNA Supermicro shows off Vera Rubin NVL72 rack with all-new type of coolant AMD, Broadcom, and Nvidia join hyperscalers to define optical scale-up interconnect of the future for AI clusters But there is a major downside with using Ethernet, even with the UALink protocol on top: Ethernet was originally designed as a general-purpose networking technology; it was never designed to scale up AI accelerators.As a result, communications may involve higher latency, more protocol overhead, and less deterministic performance than a dedicated scale-up fabric. For large AI training jobs that need all 72 Instinct MI455X accelerators to work in concert, communication efficiency is as important as compute performance. If the UALink-over-Ethernet interconnect cannot keep GPUs fed with data efficiently, some of the theoretical performance of the hardware may be lost in real-world deployments, even though on paper, Helios with UALink-over-Ethernet is as good as Nvidia’s NVL72 VR200 in scale-up bandwidth. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)This begs the question of whether UALink will ever be widely used with Helios and whether UALink will ever be widely deployed using copper. Hyperscalers and other companies deploying high-end AI hardware at scale rarely upgrade their hardware.While the Instinct MI455X certainly promises to be among the best hardware accelerators this year, Helios will likely only be rivalled by Nvidia’s NVL72 VR200. It will be outdated next year when AMD launches its Instinct MI500-series products. These units will be used in the company’s next-generation rack-scale offering, which promises to pack more AI GPUs, potentially requiring optical interconnects with UALink on top. As a result, Helios systems with true UALink interconnections over copper will be on the market for less than a year before those next-generation rack-scale solutions will hit the market.Of course, nothing is stopping AMD from offering Helios with Instinct MI500-series accelerators and UALink interconnects over copper; however, the company hasn't confirmed the existence of such systems.
history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: AMD的Helios MI455X AI平台曝光，首批系统使用基于以太网的UALink互连——AMD的Vera Rubin竞争对手浮出水面，但以太网的缺点可能限制性能
history.descriptionZh:
AMD在2026年台北国际电脑展上展示其下一代Helios机架级AI系统，多家合作伙伴推出了基于AMD EPYC“Venice”处理器和Instinct MI455X AI加速器的解决方案，预计今年晚些时候上市。这批系统存在一个关键限制：它们均采用UALink-over-Ethernet扩展连接（scale-up connectivity），这可能会在依赖连接性能的特定工作负载中限制其表现。不过，配备“真正”UALink互连的Helios系统后续也会推出。

Helios是AMD首款机架级AI系统，旨在对标英伟达基于下一代Vera Rubin平台的NVL72 VR200机器。该系统搭载第6代EPYC Venice CPU（最多256核心），集成72个Instinct MI455X加速器，配备总计31 TB的HBM4内存和1400 TB/s的带宽。AMD估算其性能约为2900 FP4密集PFLOPS，在计算性能上落后于英伟达的VR200 NVL72，但在HBM4内存容量上领先，这让Helios在内存密集型工作负载（如运行大型语言模型）中具有优势。

AI加速器通过UALink-over-Ethernet连接互连，提供高达260 TB/s的聚合扩展带宽（与英伟达NVL72 VR200持平）。Helios还配备了Pensando Vulcano网络接口卡（NICs），这是业界首批符合超以太网（Ultra Ethernet）规范的800 GbE网卡之一，可提供高达43 TB/s的扩展网络（scale-out）带宽。

然而，这些Helios系统使用的互连方案将有所不同。机器同时支持UALink和UALink-over-Ethernet，但初始版本将使用后者而非前者。原因很可能是UALink交换机尚未最终确定，且仍在等待AMD AI客户的验证和资格认证。使用UALink-over-Ethernet的最大优势在于，AMD可以利用现有且广泛支持的已验证合格组件生态系统来构建Helios。以太网交换ASIC、线缆及其他组件已被全球超大规模云提供商和云服务商广泛使用，从而加速部署。

但使用以太网（即使在其上叠加UALink协议）也存在重大缺陷：以太网最初设计为通用网络技术，从未为扩展AI加速器而设计。因此，通信可能带来更高的延迟、更多的协议开销以及比专用扩展网络更低的确定性性能。对于需要全部72个Instinct MI455X加速器协同工作的大型AI训练任务，通信效率与计算性能同样重要。如果UALink-over-Ethernet互连无法高效地为GPU提供数据，即使理论上Helios在扩展带宽上与英伟达NVL72 VR200相当，实际部署中硬件的一部分理论性能也可能损失。

这引出一个问题：UALink是否会被广泛用于Helios，以及UALink是否会通过铜缆广泛部署。超大规模云提供商和其他大规模部署高端AI硬件的公司很少升级其硬件。虽然Instinct MI455X无疑是今年最好的硬件加速器之一，但Helios可能只与英伟达的NVL72 VR200竞争。明年AMD推出Instinct MI500系列产品后，Helios将面临过时。这些新单元将用于公司下一代机架级解决方案，承诺集成更多AI GPU，可能需要基于UALink的光互连。因此，配备真正铜缆UALink互连的Helios系统在市场上存在的时间将不到一年，之后下一代机架级解决方案就会上市。当然，AMD并未排除提供配备Instinct MI500系列加速器和铜缆UALink互连的Helios系统的可能性，但公司尚未确认此类系统的存在。

history.title: $559 Nvidia RTX 5070 GPU deal is the cheapest model available — 1440p high-performance gaming at just $10 above MSRP
history.description: Quickly grab the cheapest RTX 5070 GPU available right now. Lenovo deal strips $80 off the list price.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Recent updates Sold out: As expected, the $559 RTX 5070 has sold out at Lenovo. Your next best best for this card is Walmart's $599 PNY variant, which is just $40 more. I'm not sure how long this deal will be around, as it's currently the cheapest RTX 5070 GPU available. You can grab yourself the latest MSI GeForce RTX 5070 Shadow 3X OC graphics card from Lenovo for $559.99. If you've been on the hunt for a new GPU and have been keeping an eye on prices, you already know that this current deal is $40 cheaper than the next lowest-priced RTX 5070 card, and $80 cheaper than its usual list price. It's $10 more than the original MSRP launch price, but in the current climate, that's pretty good, as this card hasn't dipped under that threshold since Black Friday last year. Check out this deal at Lenovo The RTX 5070 is a great card for 1440p gaming, and can even dip its toes into 4K gaming, but you might need a little help from DLSS to balance out the framerates at the highest resolutions. The card comes with 12GB of GDDR7 VRAM, which is adequate for most game titles, but can be easily absorbed at higher resolutions on graphics-heavy games. So some graphical settings tweaking might need to take place to optimise performance in some titles. sold outSave 13% ($80)MSI GeForce RTX 5070 Shadow 3X OC 12GB: was $639.99 now $559.99 at Lenovo USAThe RTX 5070 Shadow 3X OC sports 6,144 CUDA cores and packs 12GB of GDDR7 VRAM. This card comes factory overclocked with a triple-fan cooling solution. Connect to your monitor via any of the three DisplayPorts or the HDMI interface. View Deal In our benchmark testing of the RTX 5070, we found the GPU to have slight generational improvements over the previous-gen RTX 4070, sitting just below the middle of the stack behind its more powerful brethren, the 5070 Ti. The latest 50-series cards are also able to make use of Nvidia's DLSS and MFG features to enhance performance through a software boost. Image 1 of 4(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware) The RTX 5070 is based on a cut-down GB205 GPU with 48 enabled Streaming Multiprocessors (SMs) for a total of 6,144 CUDA cores. The GPU packs 12GB of GDDR7 memory and a TBP (Total Board Power) of 250W. The Shadow 3X OC comes factory overclocked with a triple-fan cooling solution. For video connectivity, there are three DisplayPorts and one HDMI interface.This isn't the cheapest ever price for an RTX 5070 GPU, but at $559, this MSI Shadow 3X OC RTX 5070 is currently the cheapest one going. The only real concern with this GPU is the amount of VRAM. 12GB is more than enough for most tasks and games, but there are some titles that will exceed 12GB, and this limits future-proofing. But with the current AI squeeze on PC components, the future may well have to look to game optimization, rather than throwing precious and expensive memory at GPUs. history.author: Stewart Bendle
history.titleZh: $559 Nvidia RTX 5070 GPU deal is the cheapest model available — 1440p high-performance gaming at just $10 above MSRP
history.descriptionZh:
RTX 5070作为NVIDIA新一代中端显卡，近期在联想官网以559.99美元的价格促销（MSI Shadow 3X OC版本），相比原价639.99美元节省了80美元（约13%），比官方建议零售价仅高出10美元。该促销已告售罄，目前市面最低价是沃尔玛599美元的PNY版本。这款显卡基于经过削减的GB205 GPU核心，拥有48个流式多处理器（SM），总计6144个CUDA核心，搭配12GB GDDR7显存，总板卡功耗（TBP）为250W。MSI Shadow 3X OC版本出厂即超频，采用三风扇散热方案，视频输出接口包括三个DisplayPort和一个HDMI。

技术原理上，RTX 5070支持NVIDIA的DLSS和MFG（多帧生成）功能，通过软件增强实现性能提升。在Tom's Hardware的基准测试中，该卡相较上一代RTX 4070仅有小幅代际提升，处于RTX 50系列中端偏下位置，性能弱于RTX 5070 Ti。游戏性能方面，RTX 5070非常适合1440p分辨率游戏，也可涉足4K游戏，但需要借助DLSS来平衡帧率。12GB GDDR7显存对于多数游戏标题足够，但在高分辨率、高图形负载的游戏中可能被快速消耗，需要适当调整图形设置以优化性能。部分游戏会超过12GB显存占用，这限制了未来的升级空间。

在AI芯片供应紧张的背景下，当前显卡价格受AI算力需求挤压，未来游戏可能更依赖优化而非堆砌昂贵显存。该显卡性价比在目前市场环境中尚可，但显存容量是其主要顾虑。用户若寻找更多优惠，可关注各类硬件促销页面。

history.title: Frore shows off LiquidJet Nexus coldplate for Nvidia Vera Rubin, other AI accelerators — offers up claimed 10% token generation boost over rival liquid-cooling solutions
history.description: Frore’s LiquidJet Nexus promises to enable 10% more token generation on Blackwell Ultra when compared to existing liquid-cooling solutions.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Frore Systems is showing off its LiquidJet Nexus at Computex 2026 in Taipei, Taiwan. The LiquidJet Nexus is a monolithic water block with innovative coldplates, designed to cool two Blackwell GPUs and a Grace CPU, that can replace the complex water block used today. Based on tests conducted by an ODM, the LiquidJet Nexus outperforms the default cooling solution used today and reduces GPU temperatures by around 6ºC, which increases token generation by 10%. Frore intends to build LiquidJet Nexus for Nvidia’s Rubin platforms and is ready to produce them for other accelerators, too. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Earlier this year, Frore introduced its LiquidJet, a coldplate for AI accelerators, tailor-made for exact models of processors using tools originally meant to make semiconductors, in a bid to maximize cooling performance. The LiquidJet Nexus water block builds upon these principles, integrating them into a monolithic unit designed to cool down both GPUs and a CPU inside a server tray. For now, Frore is demonstrating LiquidJet Nexus for Nvidia’s Grace Blackwell superchip, though a version compatible with Vera Rubin is also incoming, we're told.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Frore’s LiquidJet coldplates are made using tools designed to produce semiconductors — using etching and bonding steps — and are architected in accordance with actual thermal maps of CPUs and GPUs they are meant to cool. As a result, they remove heat precisely from hotspots of these processors, and therefore enable better cooling performance than coldplates made using traditional milling methods. Based on tests conducted by a major ODM, Frore’s LiquidJet Nexus reduces the temperature of Blackwell GPUs by 6ºC compared to default cooling solutions, which in turn increases their token generation by 10%.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: (Image credit: Frore Systems)While a 6ºC temperature drop and a 10% performance improvement may sound humble, these performance improvements impact billion-dollar deployments, where such improvements could mean hundreds of millions in savings. Also, since Frore’s Liquid Jet Nexus is monolithic, it is less prone to leakage. This means less downtime and fewer damaged servers, which means more profits and fewer losses for their owners. Again, since we are talking about billion-dollar deployments, there are significant amounts of money at stake when it comes to cooling and efficiency. (Image credit: Frore Systems)Frore says it is working with the majority of hyperscalers to build LiquidJet-based cooling solutions for their custom hardware. Since LiquidJet is designed to remove 400W – 600W of thermal energy per square centimeter, it can cool down very hot components. Furthermore, since these components tend to scale horizontally, it is not a problem to scale LiquidJet’s performance by increasing its dimensions. You may like Supermicro shows off Vera Rubin NVL72 rack with all-new type of coolant Noctua shows off improved thermosiphon prototype Nvidia announces Vera Rubin Space Module In addition to being more performant and potentially significantly more reliable than existing liquid-cooling solutions for Nvidia Blackwell, Frore’s LiquidJet Nexus also weighs 65% less than rivals and is twice as thin (17 mm vs 34 mm), according to Frore.While this may not be a significant advantage today (unless you ship your servers by plane), this will be a dramatic advantage for Nvidia’s next-generation Kyber chassis that places servers on their edge rather than horizontally, which will make the importance of LiquidJet Nexus’s weight a bigger factor, as the cooler must adhere to the cooling surface of the integrated heat spreader thoroughly. Meanwhile, it is hard to adhere a massive cooler to a vertically standing motherboard, so one with a lower weight should be easier to attach to the motherboard and chassis without worrying about longer-term deformations.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Frore Systems)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Speaking of Nvidia’s Kyber chassis, it is worth noting that they are designed for the Vera Rubin Ultra platform, which ups the TDP of GPUs all the way to around 3kW per unit, making its cooling a challenge. Meanwhile, Rubin Ultra GPU scales horizontally by employing a quad-chiplet design, so Frore can address its TDP by reinventing its coldplate, which is easy assuming the company is provided a thermal map of the unit. The same method can be applied to other processors, which is why Frore is indeed working with hyperscalers with custom silicon, in addition to other merchant silicon providers, aside from Nvidia.
history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: Frore展示为英伟达Vera Rubin及其他AI加速器设计的LiquidJet Nexus冷板 — 声称相比竞品液冷方案可提升10%的令牌生成性能
history.descriptionZh:
在2026年台北Computex展会上，Frore Systems展示了其创新的LiquidJet Nexus水冷块。这是一款专为英伟达Grace Blackwell超级芯片设计的一体化水冷解决方案，能够同时冷却两颗Blackwell GPU和一颗Grace CPU，取代目前复杂的多组件水冷系统。更令人关注的是，Frore透露也正在开发适配英伟达下一代Vera Rubin平台（包括Rubin Ultra）的版本，并计划为其他AI加速器提供同类方案。

**核心技术原理与创新点**

LiquidJet Nexus的核心创新在于其冷板的制造工艺。与传统的机械铣削方法不同，Frore采用半导体制造中使用的蚀刻和键合工具来制作冷板。这意味着冷板的微通道结构可以完全根据目标处理器的实际热分布图（thermal map）进行定制——哪里发热最集中，就在哪里布置更密集的微流道。这种“按需散热”的设计使得热量能够被精准地从芯片热点区域移除，显著提升了散热效率。此外，LiquidJet Nexus采用单片式（monolithic）结构，将所有通道集成在一块水冷块内，相比多个分离组件连接而成的传统水冷方案，大大降低了泄漏风险。

**性能参数与对比数据**

根据一家主要ODM（原始设计制造商）的测试，使用LiquidJet Nexus后，Blackwell GPU的温度比默认冷却方案降低了约6°C。温度降低直接带来了性能提升——在处理AI推理任务时，token生成速度提高了10%。乍看之下，6°C和10%的提升似乎不大，但对于投资动辄数十亿美元的AI数据中心，这意味着数百万美元的电力节省和效率提升。此外，Frore宣称LiquidJet Nexus比竞品轻65%，厚度仅为17毫米（竞品为34毫米），仅为一半。重量和厚度的优势在当前水平放置的服务器中尚不显著，但对于英伟达下一代Kyber机箱（将服务器竖立放置，冷板需垂直贴合集成散热器）尤为重要——较轻的冷板能更好地固定在主板上，避免在长期运行中因重力导致变形或接触不良。

**技术影响与应用场景**

LiquidJet Nexus的散热能力极为突出：每平方厘米可带走400W至600W的热量，足以应对当前最热的高性能芯片。由于AI加速器通常采用水平扩展（即通过增加芯片数量而非单芯片性能提升来扩大算力），Frore的方案可以通过等比增大冷板尺寸来匹配更大面积的芯片组，无需改变核心工艺。例如，英伟达将在Rubin Ultra平台中采用四芯片集成设计，单颗GPU的TDP将飙升至约3千瓦，Frore只需获取其热分布图即可重新设计冷板，应对极高的散热挑战。

目前，Frore正在与多家超大规模云服务商合作，为其定制基于LiquidJet的散热方案。除了英伟达，Frore也在与其它商用芯片供应商合作，甚至包括拥有自研芯片（custom silicon）的超大规模客户。这意味着这项技术不仅服务于英伟达生态，更可能成为下一代高功耗AI服务器的通用散热标准。从商业角度看，更高的散热效率、更低的泄漏风险、更少的停机时间，以及当部署规模达数十亿美元时因性能提升带来的收益，都使得LiquidJet Nexus成为AI基础设施中一个至关重要的技术突破。

history.title: Microsoft CEO says new AI data centers use as little water annually as a restaurant — closed-loop cooling system aims to slash consumption from millions of gallons as AI infrastructure faces mounting environmental scrutiny
history.description: Microsoft CEO claims the company's new AI data centers use only as much water as a single restaurant annually, thanks to a closed-loop cooling system designed to dramatically reduce consumption.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has claimed that the company's newest generation of AI data centers uses so little water that its annual consumption is roughly equivalent to that of a single restaurant. Speaking at Microsoft Build 2026 on June 2, Nadella made the claim while outlining Microsoft's "Community-First AI Infrastructure" strategy aimed to address growing concerns about the impact of data centers on local communities."The cooling loop is filled once, and the data center can operate effectively with zero water consumption," Nadella said during his keynote, while describing how new architectures are redefining data center water consumption. "The daily water usage over the course of an entire year is roughly equivalent to what a single restaurant would use."Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowWhile Nadella did not specify the size or type of restaurant used for comparison, the claim is striking given the scale of modern hyperscale AI facilities. Industry estimates suggest that restaurants can consume anywhere from several hundred thousand to more than a million gallons of water annually, while 100MW+ hyperscale data centers have historically required tens to hundreds of millions of gallons of water annually for cooling, depending on climate, workload, and cooling design.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Nadella's statement — not the first time Microsoft has used a restaurant comparison — is based on Microsoft's new closed-loop liquid-cooling architecture. Traditional data centers often rely on evaporative cooling systems that continuously consume water to remove heat generated by servers and networking equipment. In Microsoft’s new approach, over 90% of the facility's cooling relies on a closed-loop liquid cooling system that is filled during construction and then continuously recirculates the same water rather than constantly consuming a fresh supply. The remaining portion of the cooling system primarily relies on outside air and only uses additional water in the hottest conditions.The system works by circulating cooled water through heat exchangers connected to the AI hardware. As the water absorbs heat, it is routed to a massive chiller plant where large cooling fans dissipate the heat before the water is recirculated back through the facility. Because the water remains in the cooling loop rather than evaporating and being discarded, ongoing water consumption is dramatically reduced. This can translate to savings of multiple billions of gallons of water across Microsoft’s data centers. You may like Amazon, Microsoft, and Google under investor pressure to disclose data center water and power use AI data center project secretly sucked 29 million gallons of water over 15 months before detected by residents complaining about low water pressure US startup plans to build data centers inside ocean-based wind turbines, servers water cooled via chilly North Sea The current reality is much more subdued. The new cooling design and its water savings are currently implemented only at Microsoft's Fairwater AI data center campus in Mount Pleasant, Wisconsin. On the other hand, the company has stated that the new cooling approach will become the standard design for future AI-focused data centers. Microsoft has confirmed that multiple identical Fairwater facilities are already under construction elsewhere in the United States as part of a broader expansion of its AI infrastructure footprint. The rollout forms part of Microsoft's longer-term goal of becoming water positive by 2030, meaning the company aims to replenish more water than it consumes globally, as part of its community-first strategy.While the new cooling design will substantially reduce water use in future facilities, critics note that Microsoft already operates a vast global network of data centers. Azure now spans more than 500 facilities across 80 regions worldwide, many of which were built before Fairwater's cooling architecture was introduced.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsMicrosoft has not announced a comprehensive retrofit program that would convert existing data centers to the new closed-loop design. As a result, the "restaurant-level" water footprint applies primarily to newly built facilities that use the Fairwater blueprint, rather than to Microsoft's entire global infrastructure estate.The debate reflects a broader challenge facing the AI industry. Companies, including Microsoft and Google, are racing to build ever-larger AI clusters while facing mounting public pressure to reduce their environmental impact, as communities grow increasingly disapproving of large-scale AI infrastructure projects. Google recently announced a series of water stewardship commitments of its own, including a pledge to replenish more water than its data centers consume by 2030. history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: 微软CEO称新AI数据中心年用水量仅相当于一家餐厅——面对日益严格的环境审查，闭环冷却系统旨在将用水量从数百万加仑大幅削减
history.descriptionZh: 在微软Build 2026开发者大会上，首席执行官萨提亚·纳德拉宣布，该公司最新一代AI数据中心的年用水量已降至相当于一家餐厅的水平。这一表述旨在回应公众对数据中心建设日益增长的社区影响担忧。纳德拉介绍了微软的“社区优先AI基础设施”战略，并详细阐述了新型数据中心冷却架构如何彻底改变水消耗模式。核心创新在于一种闭环液体冷却系统：系统在建造时一次性注入冷却液，之后通过密封回路持续循环，不再需要常规补水。超过90%的设施冷却依赖这一闭环系统；剩余的冷却部分主要利用外部空气，仅在极端高温条件下才会额外消耗少量水。具体工作原理是：冷却水流经与AI硬件相连的热交换器，吸收热量后进入大型冷机厂房，由大型冷却风扇将热量散发到空气中，之后冷却水再次循环回设施。由于水始终在回路中流动而不蒸发或排放，运行期间的用水量被大幅削减。据微软估算，这种设计可使整个公司数据中心的用水量减少数十亿加仑。纳德拉用餐厅对比来形象说明：传统餐馆年用水量在几十万到一百多万加仑之间，而同样规模的超大规模AI数据中心以往每年需要数千万到数亿加仑水用于冷却。如今，采用新架构的数据中心一年消耗的水量仅相当于一家餐厅。不过，目前这一设计仅应用于微软位于威斯康星州芒特普莱森特的“Fairwater AI数据中心园区”。微软已确认，多个相同设计的Fairwater设施正在美国其他地点建设，并将成为未来所有AI数据中心的标配设计。这是微软到2030年实现“水正效益”（即全球补水量超过消耗量）长期目标的一部分。然而，批评者指出，微软目前运营着全球500多座数据中心，分布在80多个区域，其中绝大多数是在Fairwater冷却架构推出前建造的。微软尚未宣布对现有数据中心进行大规模改造以采用新闭环设计的计划。因此，“餐厅级”水足迹目前仅适用于新建的Fairwater蓝图设施，而非全部全球基础设施。这一争议反映了AI行业面临的整体挑战：谷歌等公司也在竞相建设更大规模的AI集群，同时承受着越来越大的环保压力。谷歌最近也承诺到2030年实现数据中心的补水超过耗水。微软的新冷却技术标志着AI基础设施可持续性的重要进步，但全面落地仍需时间。
